title: TUI clip function has wrong behavior at boundary widths
description:
File: python/src/lichen/tui/native.py
Lines: 356-364

The clip function has an edge case:

    def clip(value: str, width: int) -> str:
        if width <= 0:
            return ""
        if len(value) <= width:
            return value
        if width <= 3:
            return value[:width]  # No ellipsis for very small widths
        return f"{value[: width - 3]}..."

When width=4 and value has length 5+:
- Returns value[:1] + "..." = 1 char + 3 dots = 4 chars. Correct.

When width=3 and value has length 4+:
- Returns value[:3] with no ellipsis. This truncates without indication.

When width=2 and value has length 3+:
- Returns value[:2] with no ellipsis.

The behavior at width<=3 silently truncates without ellipsis, which could mislead users into thinking they're seeing the full value.

Impact: User confusion when terminal is very narrow - text truncated without visual indicator.

Fix: Either show ".." for width=3, "." for width=2, or don't truncate at all for very narrow widths (return "" or the full value and let the terminal handle it).
